content.problemStatement:
Logical operations can be combined using parentheses for grouping to form complex logical expressions.

Consider the snippet below:
```python
bool_array1 = np.array([True, False, True, False])
bool_array2 = np.array([False, False, True, True])

combined = np.logical_or(np.logical_and(bool_array1, bool_array2), np.logical_not(bool_array1))
print(combined)       # Output - [False  True  True  True]
```
- `np.logical_and(bool_array1, bool_array2)` performs an element-wise logical AND operation. \
Output of this is `[False, False, True, False]`
- `np.logical_not(bool_array1)` returns the array - `[False, True, False, True]`
- `np.logical_or` of the above 2 arrays will return `[False  True  True  True]`

We have populated a code to show combined logical operations in 2D arrays.\
Check out the output of this code - try and understand the working of the code.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
